questions.question: Define the key components of a block cipher.
keyPoints: Substitution-Permutation Network (SPN)
S-Boxes
P-Boxes
Key Schedule

questions.question: Explain briefly the design considerations for a block cipher.
keyPoints: Security
Performance
Key size
Block size

questions.question: Compare and contrast different block ciphers (e.g., AES, DES, Blowfish) in terms of their design trade-offs and security features.
keyPoints: Key size and block size comparison
Number of rounds comparison
Security features comparison (e.g., resistance to differential, linear, and side-channel attacks)
Design trade-offs (e.g., security vs. performance)

questions.question: Explain with example the importance of key size and block size in ensuring the security of a block cipher.
keyPoints: Definition of key size and block size
Impact of small key size and block size on security
Example of a block cipher with sufficient key size and block size (e.g., AES)
Explanation of how larger key size and block size improve security

questions.question: Justify the use of a sufficient number of rounds in a block cipher to ensure security.
keyPoints: Definition of a round in a block cipher
Impact of insufficient number of rounds on security
Explanation of how a sufficient number of rounds improves security
Example of a block cipher with a sufficient number of rounds (e.g., AES)
